questions.q: What is the primary benefit of exporting the variable `export do='--dry-run=client -o yaml'` during an exam?
options: It forces kubectl to skip validation checks.
It allows you to quickly generate base YAML manifests without memorising the syntax.
It automatically applies the YAML to the cluster.
It tells the API server to perform a dry run.
questions.explanation: By appending `$do` to an imperative command (e.g. `kubectl run pod --image=nginx $do > pod.yaml`), you instantly generate a perfectly formatted YAML file without actually creating the resource, allowing you to edit it for complex questions.

questions.q: Why is it important to use `--force --grace-period=0` when deleting pods in an exam?
options: It ensures the pod's data is backed up before deletion.
It deletes the pod immediately without waiting for the default 30-second graceful termination, saving valuable exam time.
It bypasses finalizers that might block deletion.
It prevents the Deployment from recreating the pod.
questions.explanation: Time management is critical in Kubernetes exams. By default, pods take 30 seconds to terminate gracefully. Using `--force --grace-period=0` kills the pod immediately, allowing you to recreate it without waiting.
